Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Robert Goldberg To Provide Further Responses To Request To Produce Documents And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-06-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:

Shawn Zaman, Esq. (818-290-3810; szaman@weisszaman.com) of WEISS & ZAMAN for Plaintiff.
KELLAN S. PATTERSON, Esq. (916.905.4464; Info@kellanpatterson.com) for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE'S Motion To Compel Defendant Robert Goldberg To Provide Further Responses To Request To Produce Documents And Request For Sanctions.

Plaintiff's Motion to Compel RFPs 1, 9, 10, 12, 13, 14, 17, 18, 19, 28, 29, and 30 is granted and denied as follows: As to RFPs 1, 9, 10, 12, 13, 14, 18, 28, and 29 the motions is denied. The objection that the documents requested are not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence is sustained. No evidence was presented that links the case referenced in the RFPs (CGC-19-581942) to the current dispute. No evidence was presented regarding Defendant being interviewed by people from London regarding Donald McGuire. The Motion is granted as to RFPs 17, 19, 30. All objections (other than the attorney-client privilege and attorney work product objections) are overruled. Documents responsive to these requests shall be produced within two weeks of notice of this order. The attorney-client privilege objection is granted. Any and all documents withheld on the basis of attorney-client privilege shall be listed in a privilege log. All requests for sanctions are denied.

Prevailing party to submit proposed orders to JPT via email with cc to opposing party/counsel: peter.catalanotti@wilsonelser.com

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: A. Datt, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT

MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION
Off Calendar Dec-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Shawn Zaman, Esq. (818.290.3810; szaman@weisslawla.com) of Weiss & Zaman for Plaintiff.
Kellan Patterson, Esq. (916.905.4464; info@kellanpatterson.com) for Defendant, Robert Goldberg.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE's MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION:

Plaintiff John Doe's Motion to Compel Defendant Robert Goldberg's Attendance at Deposition is GRANTED. The deposition shall take place within three weeks of notice of this order. The parties are ordered to meet and confer on the following with regard to the deposition: (1) date and time, (2) location, and (3) whether virtual appearances are acceptable. If the deposition does not go forward within three weeks of notice of this order, sanctions in the amount of $1,500 are due and payable within four weeks of notice of this order by Defendant Goldberg in favor of Plaintiff John Doe.

Mr. Zaman and Mr. Patterson to discuss and submit a stipulated proposed order via email to Peter.Catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
